commit.message:
Force dynamic rendering on circle and profile pages to fix build

The /circles pages query new database tables that don't exist during
the Replit build step (migration runs after build). Adding
force-dynamic prevents Next.js from trying to statically generate
these pages at build time.

feat: Show fork sessions with parent lead name/color and git fork icon [Midtown !2042] (#1777)

* feat: Show fork sessions with parent lead's name/color and git fork icon

Fork sessions in thread panel now display the parent channel lead's
name and avatar color instead of the raw "fork-XXXX" session name.
A git-fork icon badge replaces the old blue dot indicator to clearly
mark forked messages.

Backend: Added parent_lead field to ThreadOwnershipData, populated
from the channel lead's session name in all fork/unfork/query paths.

Frontend: New threadForkParents store tracks parent lead names.
MessageRow uses displayName/displayColor derived from forkParentLead
prop, with isForkWithParent gating the display swap and icon badge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback for fork session display

- Fix #1: Show git-fork icon fallback when parent_lead is unresolvable
  (isDedicatedSession gates icon, not isForkWithParent)
- Fix #2: Narrow isDedicatedSession check to msg.from === forkOwner
  to prevent misattributing coworker messages to parent lead
- Fix #3: Avatar click handler uses displayName instead of msg.from
- Fix #4: Resolve parent_lead via channel_lead_sessions in
  handle_session_fork (not calling_session_id)
- Fix #5: Clear stale threadForkParents when has_dedicated_session
  is true but parent_lead is absent

  and frontend store documentation

Same pattern as the threadForkParents fix — when has_dedicated_session
is true but owner is absent, clear any stale entry to prevent
misattribution of fork activity dots.

commit.message:
fix: add ts-node and correct areaHash test fixture

ts-node was missing from devDependencies — required by Jest to parse
jest.config.ts. Added ts-node ^10.9.0.

areaHash test: the 'same grid cell' fixture used coordinates that straddle
the 37.775 rounding boundary (37.7749 → 37.77, 37.7752 → 37.78), so they
hashed to different cells despite being ~30m apart. The implementation is
correct — toAreaHash rounds to 2 decimal places as specified. Fixed the
fixture to use coordinates that are unambiguously within the same cell.

All 224 tests pass across 22 suites.

fix(webauthn): Auth fallback when passkey already exists on device

- If registration fails (passkey already on device), try authenticating
  with the existing passkey as fallback instead of just showing error
- Replace fixed 500ms timeout with session polling loop (max 2.5s)
- Add server-side logging in validate + register for debugging
- Better Spanish error messages for each failure mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: kill orphaned signal-cli JVMs on startup

Multiple java.exe processes accumulate from previous daemon sessions and
hold the signal-cli config lock, causing subprocess receive calls to fail.
On _start_polling, use wmic to find and kill only java.exe processes with
signal-cli in their command line.

commit.message:
feat(security): add CVE-2024-3660 Lambda code injection attribution (#604)

* feat(security): add CVE-2024-3660 attribution to Lambda layer detection

CVE-2024-3660 (CVSS 9.8): Keras Lambda layers allow arbitrary code injection
during model deserialization. Adds explicit CVE attribution to existing
Lambda detection in both .keras ZIP and .h5 scanners.

- Add CVE-2024-3660 CRITICAL check for all Lambda layers in both scanners
- Add explanation function for CVE-2024-3660
- 2 new tests (attribution present, no false positive without Lambda)
- Update existing safe Lambda test for CVE-2024-3660 behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: consolidate duplicate [Unreleased] CHANGELOG section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add layer context to CVE-2024-3660 H5 scanner attribution

Include layer_name in details, message, and location for consistency
with the ZIP scanner's CVE-2024-3660 detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(keras): gate CVE-2024-3660 attribution by keras version

* fix: add missing type annotations and merge duplicate CHANGELOG sections

- Add `from pathlib import Path` import to test_keras_zip_scanner.py
- Annotate `tmp_path: Path` in `_make_keras_zip` to satisfy mypy disallow_incomplete_defs
- Move [Unreleased] section to top of CHANGELOG (before [0.2.26]) and merge both [Unreleased] sections into one, combining ### Added, ### Security, and ### Fixed subsections

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: trigger CI run

* test(keras): use pathlib in CVE-2024-3660 zip helpers

* test: tighten CVE-2024-3660 attribution coverage

* fix: remove duplicate Unreleased section with merge conflict artifacts from CHANGELOG

The CHANGELOG had a second [Unreleased] section with leftover merge conflict
markers from a previous merge. The CVE-2024-3660 entry was already properly
placed in the first [Unreleased] section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle 2-part versions and None layer names in H5 scanner

Address CodeRabbit review feedback on PR #604:

1. _is_vulnerable_to_cve_2024_3660() now handles 2-part versions like
   "2.10" by padding missing patch with 0, matching the ZIP scanner fix.
2. layer_name uses `get("name") or fallback` to handle keys with None
   values instead of `get("name", fallback)`.

commit.message:
fix(spray): close orphaned tokens on restart

Tokens left open in DB from a previous process will never be closed
by the new exit loop (they're not in memory). On init, mark any
DB tokens with NULL exit_reason as 'restart_orphan' with 0 P&L.

The unlock button was calling the baseline toggle API which changed both
bloqueado AND esBaseline together, preventing proper unlock. Now uses a
dedicated /bloqueo endpoint that only toggles the lock state. Also
expanded unlock permissions from admin-only to admin/gerente/gestor/coordinador.

show swipe: fix empty state - relax quality filter for trending, try more pages (#69)

Trending/now_playing are already curated by TMDB — our vote_count >= 50
filter was killing most new shows (Young Sherlock: 11 votes, Marshals: 34,
etc). Now only discover uses strict quality gates. Also try 5 pages across
10 page range instead of 3/5.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: per-inner-call rule validation for executeBatch in RULE_ID_GENERIC (#109)

Add _validateBatchRules to validate each inner call in executeBatch separately when RULE_ID_GENERIC is used. This prevents bypassing org rules by whitelisting executeBatch at account level. Supports both PasskeyAccount (0x47e1da2a) and SimpleAccount (0x18dfb3c7) batch signatures. Expand tests from 2 to 20+ cases covering all-allowed batches, denied inner calls, edge cases (empty/short calldata), and realistic org member operations.

commit.message:
fix: v0.4.3 — menu overlap, back button, progress tracking, band score

- Menu button overlap: add padding-top: 60px to .main-content at
  mobile breakpoint so fixed menu button no longer covers page content
- Back button: replace text-link style with outlined secondary button
  (border + hover color); replace arrow char with ChevronLeft icon
- Progress tracking: after AI reading submit, calculate study minutes
  from startTime and estimated band score (3.5 + accuracy*4.5 rounded
  to nearest 0.5); call progressAPI.updateProgress with all fields +
  progressAPI.createSession for streak/daily tracking

commit.message:
fix: Correct Viper fighter geometry, optimize cameras (Issue #113 A3) (#115)

## Summary

Iterates on the Viper fighter Blender script
(`generate_viper_fighter.py`) to better match Fighter Ship v4 concept
art, as part of Issue #113 Phase A Task A3.

### Geometry fixes
- **Wing root offset**: Wings now extend from X=0 outward (4 distinct
wings) instead of centered at ±span/2 (which created 2 overlapping pairs
after rotation)
- **Y-axis rotation sign**: Fixed Blender convention — `R_y(θ)` gives
`Z' = -sin(θ)·X`, not `+sin(θ)·X`. Upper-right wings now correctly go UP
- **Weapon barrel positions**: Fixed `_get_wing_tip_positions()` to use
`-sin(θ)` matching actual wing tip coordinates

### Parameter tuning
- Wing chord: 1.5m → 2.0m root (better visibility)
- Wing angles: ±30° → ±20° (less vertical separation in side view)
- Nacelle diameter: 1.2m → 0.85m (less dominant vs fuselage)
- Nacelle spacing: 1.5m → 1.2m, rear extension: 1.0m → 0.3m
- Dorsal fin height: 0.8m → 1.2m
- Removed nacelle intake and detail torus rings (reduced visual clutter)

### Camera optimization
- 3/4 view: flipped from rear-quarter `(22,-30,15)` to front-quarter
`(15,20,6)` so nose is in foreground
- Side view: true right-side profile `(30,0,2)` with 150mm telephoto

### Key finding: Validator non-determinism

The LLM-based visual validator (`validate_visual.py`) exhibits **extreme
non-determinism**. The **identical 3/4 render** scored:
- **94%** (1 run), **81%** (another), **56%**, **50%**, **44%**,
**38%**, **34%**, **19%**

This 19%-94% range on the same image makes iterative optimization
unreliable at any threshold. The validator's scores are dominated by the
LLM's random sampling, not actual image differences. Running 3x and
taking the median helps but doesn't fully address the issue.

### Side view limitation

The side profile consistently scores 0-44%. Root cause: procedural
geometry using separate Blender primitives creates visible gaps between
components (wings, nacelles, weapon barrels appear as disconnected
floating shapes). The concept art shows a cohesive integrated ship that
can't be replicated with separate floating meshes. Future fix: either
extrude wings from the hull bmesh or use boolean union to create unified
geometry.

### Included but not enabled

`create_wing_root_fairings()` — generates connecting wedges at
wing-fuselage junctions. Improves side view cohesion but adds unfamiliar
visual elements that hurt 3/4 view scores. Kept as a function for future
use.

## Test plan
- [x] `python -m py_compile
projects/unity-space-sim/blender/scripts/generate_viper_fighter.py`
- [x] `blender --background --python generate_viper_fighter.py --
--output-dir /tmp/test` generates all expected files
- [x] Visual inspection of 3/4 and side renders

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add self-evaluation: observe own patched output to detect errors

After observing demos and writing initial patches, the model re-runs
demo problems through the patched model and observes the output via
the same obs_proj → GRU pathway. This lets the meta-learner detect
discrepancies between expected and actual output, then correct patches.

Task-agnostic: no explicit error computation. The model learns to use
the difference between demo observation and self-evaluation observation
through the shared GRU. No new parameters — uses existing obs_proj/GRU.

Usage: --self-eval-steps 2

commit.message:
fix: replace server OAuth callback with client-side exchange

The server-side Route Handler used @supabase/ssr (cookie storage) but all
client components use plain supabase-js (localStorage storage). Bridging
the two was fragile and broken for Google OAuth.

The plain client already stores the PKCE code_verifier in localStorage
during signInWithOAuth, so exchangeCodeForSession called client-side reads
it automatically and stores the resulting session in localStorage — where
NavBar, GetProButton, and auth-bridge all expect to find it.
